body:
## Summary

Fixes #108. The issue reports three symptoms: no Rust releases exist, no
badges anywhere, and no `[JavaScript]` / `[Rust]` prefix in release titles or
`js-` / `rust-` prefix in tags. Each symptom has an independent root cause; all
three are fixed in this PR.

## What changed

### 1. `scripts/create-github-release.mjs` / `scripts/format-github-release.mjs` — honour `--prefix`

Both scripts received `--prefix js-` / `--prefix rust-` from the workflows but
never declared the flag in yargs, so it was silently dropped — every release
landed as plain `v<version>`, and the next Rust release would have collided
with the JS release of the same number. Now:

- Tag: `${prefix}v${version}` → `rust-v0.14.0`, `js-v0.25.4`.
- Title: `[Rust] 0.14.0`, `[JavaScript] 0.25.4` (falls back to `<version>`
  when no prefix is supplied, so the single-language behaviour is preserved).

The tag/title construction was extracted into a new pure helper
[`scripts/release-name.mjs`](scripts/release-name.mjs) with unit tests in
[`js/test/release-name.test.mjs`](js/test/release-name.test.mjs).

### 2. `.github/workflows/rust.yml` — stop skipping `auto-release`

On every push to `main`, `auto-release` reported `conclusion: skipped` with
zero steps even though `lint`, `test`, and `build` all succeeded. GitHub
Actions propagates the `skipped` status downstream when any upstream job uses
`if: always()` unless the downstream job also uses `always()` (see
https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/491). The upstream
[`rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`](https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml)
already accounts for this with

```yaml
if: |
  always() && !cancelled() &&
  github.event_name == 'push' &&
  github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
  needs.build.result == 'success'
```

which has been adopted here for both `auto-release` and `manual-release`.

### 3. `js/README.md`, `rust/README.md`, root `README.md` — badges and cross-links

Per-language READMEs with per-package-manager badges, plus two CI/CD badges
and an npm + crates.io pair on the root README. The root README now explicitly
cross-links both language folders.

### 4. Case study & release fragments

- `docs/case-studies/issue-108/` — full investigation (`README.md`,
  `timeline.md`, `requirements.md`, `root-cause.md`, `solutions.md`), raw
  GitHub API data (`issue-data.json`, `releases.json`, CI run metadata), and
  snapshots of the two upstream template workflows we compared against.
- `rust/changelog.d/108.md` — Rust changelog fragment so the next
  `auto-release` run actually produces a release.
- `js/.changeset/issue-108-language-prefix.md` — JS changeset so the next npm
  release advertises the new `--prefix` support.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test test/release-name.test.mjs` — 6/6 pass.
- [x] `bun run lint` — clean.
- [x] `bun run format:check` — clean.
- [x] YAML syntax of both workflow files validated with `pyyaml`.
- [x] `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passes on the new JS changeset.
- [ ] **Post-merge:** watch the next push-to-main run of `rust.yml`; expect
      `auto-release` to go green and produce a `rust-v0.14.0` release titled
      `[Rust] 0.14.0`. The next JS release should be tagged `js-v0.25.5` and
      titled `[JavaScript] 0.25.5`.

## Deliberately _not_ in this PR (out of scope)

- Publishing the Rust crate to crates.io. The upstream template does it, but
  the issue only asks for GitHub releases and badges; a follow-up issue would
  enable crates.io publishing and wire up the `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN` secret.
- Fixing the pre-existing secondary bug where `create-github-release.mjs`
  looks for `## <version>` headings in the root `CHANGELOG.md`, which no
  longer match the `## [<version>] - <date>` format produced by
  `collect-changelog.mjs`. This is why current releases show the fallback
  body `Release <version>`. Not triggered by #108 and worth its own issue.
- The two pre-existing failures in `test/args-parser.test.js` are unrelated
  to this PR and exist on `main`.
title: Unblock Rust releases, add language prefixes, and add per-language READMEs
